data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Shows Divergence in Funding Rates
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that the current funding rates on major centralized and decentralized exchanges (CEX and DEX) reflect a market characterized by both bearish and neutral sentiments. The funding rates for major cryptocurrencies are detailed in the accompanying chart.

Funding rates are a mechanism used by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain the balance between contract prices and the prices of the underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This system facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the trading platform collecting any fees. It serves to adjust the cost or profit of holding contracts, ensuring that contract prices remain close to the underlying asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% is considered the baseline rate. When the funding rate exceeds 0.01%, it indicates a generally bullish market sentiment. Conversely, when the funding rate falls below 0.005%, it suggests a predominantly bearish market outlook.

data.title: Ethereum PoS Network Faces Significant Queue Delays
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Ethereum Proof-of-Stake (PoS) network is experiencing substantial delays in its validator queues. The current exit queue stands at 1,024,545 ETH, which is approximately valued at $4.45 billion based on current prices. The withdrawal delay is reported to be 17 days and 19 hours.

At the same time, there is an increase in demand for staking by new validators. The entry queue has risen to 808,910 ETH, valued at about $3.51 billion. The current waiting period for new validators to activate is 14 days and 1 hour.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Uncertainty Amid Market Caution and Key Economic Data
data.description:
According to PANews, Adam, a macro researcher at Greeks. live, shared insights in a Chinese community briefing, highlighting a cautious and slightly bearish sentiment in the current market. The group generally perceives Bitcoin's recent performance as weak, with the critical price level of 108 being repeatedly tested without strong support. Members discussed the potential impact of a weekly MACD death cross, which could lead to institutional and large-scale investors exiting the market
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/29013928096193?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS
